results.title: Replace SaaS with Claude: $12,840 Saved Playbook | Manoj Saharan
results.text:
Replace SaaS with Claude: $12,840 Saved Playbook | Manoj Saharan

# How to Replace SaaS with Claude: The $12,840 Sovereign Stack Playbook

April 22, 2026 · 14 min read

Last Tuesday I looked at my subscription list and realized I had been paying $1,100 a month to software I was barely using. In 2025 alone, GoHighLevel alone charged me $13,200. I canceled every SaaS subscription that Claude could replace. Over twelve months, I got my total software spend down from $1,100 a month to pay-per-usage - around $30 to $50 depending on the month. That is a $12,840 per year cut. No team. No dev shop. Just Claude and a weekend.

TL;DR:

- Most small businesses pay $400 to $1,100 a month for SaaS they could replace with Claude plus three infrastructure tools.
- The replacements are real and specific: Resend for email, Twilio for SMS, DuckDB for CRM, Cal.com for scheduling, n8n for automation. Claude

results.title: The $420K SaaS Tax: Cut Software Spend by 73% | AltStack
results.text:
The $420K SaaS Tax: Cut Software Spend by 73% | AltStack

# The $420K SaaS Tax: How One 50-Person Team Cut Software Spend by 73%

A 50-person agency was paying $420,000/year in SaaS subscriptions. In 11 months, they replaced 9 tools with custom software and cut spend to $114K. Here's the playbook.

MN

Mustafa Najoom· Growth & Strategy

Apr 18, 2026

Research

R.

## The Bill That Broke the Camel's Back

When Meridian Collective's CFO pulled up the accounting ledger in January 2025, one line item stopped her cold: $420,317 spent on software subscriptions in the prior 12 months.

Meridian is a 50-person creative agency. They design brands, run ad campaigns, and ship digital products for mid-market clients. By revenue, they're a healthy business — about $8.4M in annual billings. But $420K on software? That's $8,406 per employee per year, or roughly 5% of total revenue

## The vector database invoice that made our CFO scream

Last September, I got called into an “urgent meeting” with our CFO. She slid a spreadsheet across the table. One line item made my stomach drop: Pinecone: $8,200/month.

Our AI-powered document search was costing us nearly $100K annually. And we were barely scratching the surface of what we wanted to build.

“Can we make this cheaper?” she asked.

I spent the next 72 hours

results.title: How I replaced $2,000/month in SaaS with a $200 Claude Code subscription.
results.author: Max Votek
results.text:
How I replaced $2,000/month in SaaS with a $200 Claude Code subscription.

# Max Votek

SubscribeSign in

# How I replaced $2,000/month in SaaS with a $200 Claude Code subscription.

Mar 23, 2026

11

6

4

Share

I built a personal CRM from scratch.

Not a prototype. Not a demo. A production system I use every day.

47 features. 13 AI tools. A vector knowledge base. LinkedIn profile enrichment. A Telegram bot as the primary interface. Automated follow-up logic. Voice memo integration. Email parsing. Trip-based contact suggestions.

It replaced my entire stack: HubSpot, Monday.com, Notion, and Zapier.

Cost: $200/month for Claude Code.

I asked Claude Code to estimate what this would cost a traditional development team.

The answer: 516 hours. $66,400. Four months. Five people: PM, PO, two developers, QA.

I did it in roughly 20 hours across several sessions.

That’s a massive cost reduc

Startup Cuts Costs by Building Internal CRM with AI Tools Instead of Salesforce, TechGig

- Software & DevOps
- 3 min read

# A startup replaced Salesforce with a $1,200 internal CRM built using AI tools

A startup named Atonom replaced its costly Salesforce CRM with an internally built system using an AI platform. This move significantly cut annual expenses from $40,000 to $1,200. The company now builds other internal tools, demonstrating a trend where AI makes custom software cheaper than subscriptions. This shift offers substantial savings for small teams.

- Published On Mar 13, 2026 at 08:42 AM IST

For many startups, CRM software becomes a necessary but expensive piece of infrastructure. One small team decided to question that assumption. Startup Atonom replaced its $40,000 per year Salesforce contract with an internally built CRM created using the AI-powered development platform L

results.text:
We paid GHL $13,200 in 2025. I just cancelled. | Manoj Saharan

GHL Exit Sovereign Stack

# We paid GHL $13,200 in 2025. I just cancelled.

March 16, 2026 · Updated April 24, 2026

8 min read

AA

Want the actual scripts — not just the architecture?

AI Avengers Lab members get the full working setup: pre-built code, campaign scripts, and weekly live builds. $89/mo.

See what's inside the Lab

March 2026. I am looking at our GHL invoice. $1,100.

That is $13,200 a year. For a CRM that owns our data, locks us into their ecosystem, and charges more every time we grow.

So we built our own.

## What we built

This is version one. We are building this in public, documenting every decision, every mistake, every win.

Here is what we actually use:

CRM: Jarvis (built on OpenClaw + DuckDB). Free. Local. We own everything.

CMS: Sanity. Pay-per-usage. We pay for what we use, not a monthly seat.

results.title: What Is Service as Software | Replace SaaS Subscriptions With Custom Platforms
results.author: Meeky Hwang
results.text:
What Is Service as Software | Replace SaaS Subscriptions With Custom Platforms

# What Is “Service as Software” and How We Use It to Replace Six-Figure SaaS Stacks

- Contact Us
- Meeky Hwang

Most companies between 50 and 100 employees are spending somewhere between $450K and $1M a year on SaaS subscriptions. We know because we’ve looked at the numbers.

The stack usually goes something like this: a CRM that’s too big for the team. A project management tool nobody fully adopted. Three different reporting dashboards that don’t talk to each other. A handful of Zapier automations holding it all together with duct tape. And a growing line item for “integration middleware” that nobody budgeted for.

Every tool made sense when it was purchased. But stacked together, they create a slow, expensive, fragile system that no single person understands end-to-end.

We think there’s a better model. We

results.title: RadiusOS TL;DR - Chad Newell
results.author: Chad Newell
results.text:
RadiusOS TL;DR - Chad Newell

# Chad Newell

SubscribeSign in

# RadiusOS TL;DR

### Building with Claude Code

Chad Newell

Jun 10, 2026

1

Share

RadiusOS is a customer record and operating system for trades and small-business owners, live at radiusos.ai with paying customers. It was built by one founder (me), directing Claude Code as the primary engineering collaborator, day by day, in public. The stack is boring on purpose. The interesting part is the operating model we collectively used.

---

## Stack at a glance

### RadiusOS Technology Stack

Framework: Next.js 16 (App Router)Server Components, async params, and Turbopack by default.

Language: TypeScript (Strict Mode)Zero type errors is the merge bar.

UI: React 19, Tailwind v4, Radix UIMobile-first and tested at 375px.

Database: Neon Postgres (Production), SQLite (Local)One schema across both environments.

ORM: Prisma 7Serve

results.title: Claude Writes the Code, Gemini Runs It: How Two Competing AIs Cut My SaaS Costs by 30x - DEV Community
results.text:
Claude Writes the Code, Gemini Runs It: How Two Competing AIs Cut My SaaS Costs by 30x - DEV Community

# Claude Writes the Code, Gemini Runs It: How Two Competing AIs Cut My SaaS Costs by 30x

I build Nokos, an AI-powered note-taking app that auto-captures conversations from 25+ AI tools. Here's the thing — the product itself runs entirely on AI, and picking the wrong model for the wrong job almost killed the economics.

This is the story of how I went from "this will never be profitable" to "break-even at 150 users" by splitting my AI stack between two competing providers.

## The Original Architecture (And Why It Was Bleeding Money)

When I first built Nokos, I used Anthropic's Claude for everything:

| Feature | Model | Cost per call |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Metadata generation | Claude Haiku | ~¥0.5 |
| AI Chat | Claude Sonnet | ~¥4.5 |
| Personal AI (RAG) | Claude Sonnet | ~¥4.5 |
|
